title: arenaria erinacea subsp. erinacea
intro: Arenaria erinacea subsp. erinacea, also known as beach strawberry or eastern beach strawberry, is a type of flowering plant in the pink family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the eastern coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Arenaria erinacea subsp. erinacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Arenaria erinacea subsp. erinacea can be prepared for medicinal use through tea infusions, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
